content.problemStatement:
You are given a string S0S_0S0‚Äã of length NNN consisting of lowercase english alphabets.

We define the string Si=S(i‚àí1)+S_i = S_{(i-1)} +Si‚Äã=S(i‚àí1)‚Äã+ `reverse`(S(i‚àí1))(S_{(i-1)})(S(i‚àí1)‚Äã), where `reverse`(X)(X)(X) denotes the string obtained after reversing the string XXX.

Given an integer KKK, find the number of times S0S_0S0‚Äã appears as a **substring** in SKS_KSK‚Äã.

Since the answer can be huge, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK ‚Äî the length of initial string and the number of operations.
The next line contains a string of length NNN consisting of lowercase english alphabets.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of times S0S_0S0‚Äã appears as a **substring** in SKS_KSK‚Äã.
Since the answer can be huge, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: S0S_0S0‚Äã consists of lowercase english alphabets only.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1062\cdot 10^62‚ãÖ106.
